description:
You are a performance-minded engineer who has just been asked to add a very small static-security check to an existing micro-benchmark project that lives in the home directory.  
The goal is to produce one (and only one) **plain-text log file** that lists every invocation of certain insecure C-library functions that the project currently contains.  
Follow the steps in sequence, making sure you keep the exact file-paths and the required output format unchanged.

Project location  
•  /home/user/perf_project

Target source files (all are already present inside the directory)  
•  main.c  
•  util.c  
•  util.h  

Insecure functions that **MUST** be reported (case–sensitive search on the literal token)  
1. strcpy  
2. gets  
3. sprintf  

Required final artefact  
•  /home/user/perf_project/security_scan.log

Format of /home/user/perf_project/security_scan.log  
1. First line (literal, no extra spaces):  
   VULN_SCAN_LOG v1.0  
2. Second line:  
   SCANNED_AT=<unix_epoch_seconds>  
   ‑ obtain the timestamp with the command `date +%s`.  
3. Third line (literal):  
   PROJECT_ROOT=/home/user/perf_project  
4. One or more findings lines, **one line per detected call**, with the comma-separated schema shown below.  
   INSECURE_CALL,<relative_path_from_PROJECT_ROOT>,<function_name>,<line_number>  
   Example (do not hard-code):  
   INSECURE_CALL,main.c,strcpy,8  
5. The very last line is a summary.  
   SUMMARY,total_insecure_calls=<N>,total_files_scanned=<M>  
   • N  = total number of INSECURE_CALL lines you created.  
   • M  = number of *source* files (*.c / *.h) that you analysed during the scan.  

Strict rules for the log  
• Every line ends with a single LF (`\n`), no CR.  
• No blank lines anywhere in the file.  
• Values are separated by a single ASCII comma “,” — no spaces before or after.  
• The order of INSECURE_CALL lines does not matter, but the three header lines must appear first and in the given order, and the SUMMARY line must come last.  

Validation expectations (automated tests will check)  
1. File /home/user/perf_project/security_scan.log exists.  
2. First three lines and last line match the patterns exactly as described.  
3. At least ***three*** INSECURE_CALL lines are present and they mention each of the required function names (strcpy, gets, sprintf) at least once.  
4. The relative path portion of every INSECURE_CALL line is either “main.c” or “util.c” (there are no other C sources in the project).  

High-level tasks you will need to perform  
1. Navigate to the project directory.  
2. (Optional but recommended) Build the existing code into a binary named “perfapp”; this helps to confirm that compilation still works after your changes.  
3. Search each *.c* file for the insecure functions listed above and collect their line numbers.  
4. Generate the log file in the exact format required.  
5. Double-check that the file contents conform to all the validation rules.  

Important:  
• Do not rename, move, or delete the original source files.  
• Do not create any additional files outside /home/user/perf_project.  
• The only new file you should leave behind is security_scan.log in the path stated.  
• The automated grader will ignore any terminal output you produce, so feel free to echo status messages; just ensure the log file is correct.
